items.deck: Lofty gold prices and market volatility are pushing wealthy consumers toward jewelry, especially rare colored gemstones, as handbags lose appeal.

items.title: Trump threatens attack on Iran power plants if Strait of Hormuz isn't reopened
items.deck: Iran and Israel traded attacks on Saturday as the war in the Middle East escalated into a dangerous new direction at the start of its fourth week.

items.title: Former England captain John Terry appears to back calls to ban the burqa on Instagram post about Bengali signage at a London Underground station
items.deck: Terry's apparent views were displayed on an Instagram post by Rupert Lowe, the former Reform UK MP who recently launched another political party called Restore Britain.

items.title: Top Wall Street analysts are confident about the long-term prospects of these 3 stocks
items.deck: Amid elevated tensions in the Middle East, top analysts say investors should hold these 3 names for a strong long-term return.

items.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants as Iran strikes 2 Israeli cities
items.deck: Iran launched missiles at two southern Israeli cities that lie close to the country's main nuclear research center, while President Trump gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Shankland's return helps Hearts overcome Dundee and fills league leaders with belief ahead of title run-in
items.deck: One man doesn't make a team.

Russia is killing Telegram, its most popular messaging app, despite fears of pushback
items.deck: The Kremlin‘s tightening grip on Russian life has a new target: the country’s most popular messaging app, Telegram.
